  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Pump is pumping toward the expansion tank, violating the point-of-no-pressure-change rule.",
    "Sub-atmospheric pressure drop created at the boiler heat exchanger during pump operation.",
    "Risk of air ingestion through automatic air vents and localized boiling/cavitation."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Relocate the circulator pump to discharge away from the expansion tank connection point.",
    "Ensure the expansion tank connection (point of no pressure change) is located on the suction side of the circulator pump."
  ],
  "rationale": "In hydronic heating systems, the connection point of the expansion tank is the 'point of no pressure change' (PONPC). Pumping toward this point causes the pump's head to be subtracted from the system pressure rather than added to it, resulting in a pressure drop below atmospheric pressure in the boiler and piping. This induces air binding, cavitation, and reduced heat transfer.",
  "references": [
    "ASHRAE HVAC Systems and Equipment Handbook - Hydronic Heating and Cooling System Design",
    "Bell & Gossett (ITT) Hydronic System Design Manual",
    "Modern Hydronic Heating (John Siegenthaler)"
  ]
}
